entries.text: SESSION 2-JUL-2026: Architecture review. System assessment: bot_stable.py online, EventBus workers mismatch identified, duplicate polling resolved. Priority: STABLE CORE - single entrypoint, clean init, zero 409 by design. OPEN: create AGENT_RULES.md, populate journal, clean .bak files, verify bot.py dependencies, Dockerfile warnings, /refreshtoken decision, student onboarding.

NEXT SESSION TASKS: (1) Clean .bak files from disk. (2) Verify zero dependencies on bot.py, delete if clean. (3) Fix Dockerfile warnings. (4) Decide on /refreshtoken Railway auto-update. (5) Student onboarding - recruit 10 students. (6) smart_reply edge case - partial output before buttons.

SESSION 2-JUL-2026: Cleanup + hardening. DONE: AGENT_RULES.md created, journal initialized, 5 .bak files deleted, Dockerfile ENV BOT_TOKEN removed, .gitignore expanded. ISSUES: Railway usage limit reached (deploy blocked), token exposed in CLI history - must revoke. NEXT: replace token via @BotFather + Railway Dashboard, wait for usage reset, then deploy STABLE CORE.

SESSION 3-JUL-2026: Final stabilization. DONE: journal_read fixed (removed smart_reply), Dockerfile cleaned (ENV BOT_TOKEN removed), .gitignore expanded, old backup folders ignored, git status clean, /agent_create works in memory. OPEN: agents not persisted to db.json (volatile), /agents command hangs, start_agent_thread blocks import of bot_stable. NEXT SESSION PRIORITY: fix agent persistence to db.json, unblock /agents command, test full agent lifecycle. READY: Railway Online, Users:2, Deploy passes, security OK.

SESSION 3-JUL-2026 COMPLETED: agent persistence fixed (state/db.json + Volume), /exec runs Python, /demo with tasks/agents/guide, /inbox shows outbox, /start updated, state_manager centralizes DB access, tutor agent auto-replies. System ready for alpha users. Next: Ollama LLM integration, monetization plan.
SESSION COMPLETE 3-JUL-2026: fixed /start menu via welcome_handler.py, tutor worker replies, inbox shows outbox, /demo works, persistency stable. Ready for alpha + monetization.

SESSION 4-JUL-2026: Fixed slh_dashboard.sh token KeyError (options 1 and 2) - now falls back to BOT_TOKEN env var if db.json lacks token key, matches slh_daemon.sh pattern. Verified via live test + git push (2fb7af7). DISCOVERED RISK: dashboard health check uses pgrep -f python.*bot.py which never matches bot_stable.py, so it always reports Stopped and auto-triggers ./slh_daemon.sh - currently harmless because local bot_stable.py crashes immediately (ModuleNotFoundError: core.event_bus, plugins.task; No valid token found) but becomes a live 409-conflict generator if local deps ever get fixed. NEXT SESSION PRIORITY: fix dashboard pgrep pattern to match bot_stable.py, or remove auto-start entirely and just report status.

SESSION 4-JUL-2026 PART 2: Fixed dashboard auto-start risk - pgrep pattern now matches bot_stable.py (was matching bot.py, always false), removed automatic ./slh_daemon.sh launch entirely, relabeled as Local Bot to clarify it does not reflect Railway production status. Verified Railway container has zero local dependency on slh_dashboard.sh (only process running is bot_stable.py itself, no ps binary in container). Tested live - shows correct Not running status with no auto-launch. NOTE: dashboard DB health check (option 4) still checks stale schema fields (students, courses, admins, etc) that do not exist in current db.json schema (users, agents, tasks, memory, votes) - cosmetic, low priority, not fixed this session.

SESSION 4-JUL-2026 PART 3 - Economic engine audit: Found marketplace.py wrote to /app/marketplace.json (ephemeral container disk, NOT Volume) - active data-loss risk, would reset on every deploy. FIXED: changed STORE_FILE path to state/marketplace.json, preserved existing data (health_check, task_plugin, agent_os, junk_cleaner plugins) by copying to new location before code change. Verified live on Railway post-deploy - code and file both confirmed at new path. ALSO DISCOVERED (not yet fixed): (1) TWO competing /join handlers registered - learn_handlers.py and welcome_handler.py both claim commands=[join], likely causing referral flow to silently fail since welcome_handler writes db[students] without referral_code field that learn_handlers /referral expects. (2) welcome_handler.py and learn_handlers.py bypass state_manager.py entirely, doing raw json.load/dump on relative db.json path instead of using the centralized DB access built last session - same single-source-of-truth risk as journal.json had. (3) Local Termux state/db.json was significantly stale vs Railway live db.json (missing students/courses/admins keys) - must always pull live data via /exec before doing local DB work. (4) orphaned join_fix.py file present in root, unclear if still relevant. NEXT SESSION: resolve duplicate /join handlers, migrate welcome_handler.py and learn_handlers.py to use state_manager.py, decide fate of join_fix.py.

SESSION 4-JUL-2026 PART 4 - Join handler cleanup + critical bug fix: Removed dead /join code (start_join/process_name/process_group/process_goal, 58 lines) from learn_handlers.py - was unreachable since welcome_handler.init(bot) registers first in bot_stable.py load order, telebot only executes first-matching handler for a command. CRITICAL BUG FOUND during live testing: welcome_handler.py join handler used relative path db.json (not state/db.json), which does not exist on Railway (no symlink present there unlike Termux local setup) - caused silent FileNotFoundError crash on every /join call, no user-facing response at all. Not caused by our changes - pre-existing bug, only surfaced because we tested /join live for the first time this session. FIXED: changed all 3 db.json references in welcome_handler.py to state/db.json. Verified live - /join now correctly responds. NOTE: my_progress() function in learn_handlers.py also confirmed dead code (referenced in error messages but /myprogress command never registered) - pre-existing, not touched, flagged for future cleanup. NOTE: SyntaxWarning for invalid escape sequences in welcome_handler.py markdown text (\_ \<) - cosmetic, not blocking, low priority.

SESSION 4-JUL-2026 PART 5 - Admin menu audit found 4 duplicate handlers in bot_stable.py (start, demo, logs, refreshtoken). Telebot only runs first-registered handler, rest were dead code. Removed dead start/demo/logs duplicates (76 lines), verified live via welcome_handler.py and demo_handlers.py. MAJOR FIX: refreshtoken handlers were re-registering every reconnect loop iteration inside while True - moved outside loop entirely, matches known risk flagged in prior session memory. CRITICAL BUG FOUND (pre-existing, unrelated to our changes): refresh_token_handler.py called is_admin(message.from_user.id) passing an int, but is_admin() expects full message object - caused silent AttributeError crash on every refreshtoken call, zero user feedback. Fixed to is_admin(message). Verified live - full confirm flow works now (yes/cancel, safe abort on interrupt). Commits: 5302fb6, 460db40, 245cddf.

SESSION 4-JUL-2026 PART 6: Created /roadmap command (roadmap_handler.py) that reads ROADMAP.md and sends it via Telegram, auto-chunked to stay under 4096 char limit. Verified no existing duplicate before adding. ROADMAP.md updated with full 4-Jul session summary (9 bugs fixed) and critical working rules learned (no /tmp, no long paste blocks, no long python3 -c strings, state file Volume/git split). Since ROADMAP.md is a regular git-tracked file (not in state/), it auto-syncs to Railway on every push - no special /exec sync needed like journal/db/marketplace required. Verified live - /roadmap displays full updated content correctly.

entries.details: Changed help_handler.py to register_help(bot) pattern to avoid circular import. Added call in bot_stable.py. /help now works live.

Restructured /admin and /help menus (full command coverage). Fixed /help circular import via register_help pattern. Built payment_handler.py with native Telegram Stars (XTR) flow. Added /fakepay admin command for testing. Verified syntax, imports, and live functionality. Economy live: users can pay Stars, get credits, referral commissions auto-calculated.
Created ton_handler.py with /ton, /ton_check, /ton_rate, /ton_set_wallet. Integrates toncenter.com API for transaction verification. No credit card needed.
